items.title: Court dismisses Indira Gandhi's bid to cite IGP for contempt
items.summary: IPOH: The High Court today dismissed M. Indira Gandhi's application for leave to initiate committal proceedings against the Inspector-General of Police and several others over an alleged failure to locate her former husband in efforts to recover their daughter. "Accordingly, the court finds that the evidence relied upon by the applicant is insufficient to establish a prima facie case to grant leave for committal proceedings," she said. "The application is therefore dismissed."

items.title: MA63: 13 matters resolved, 5 under interim resolution, says Mustapha Sakmud
items.summary: Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Sabah and Sarawak Affairs) Datuk Mustapha Sakmud said another five issues had been given partial or interim resolution following decisions made at the MA63 Technical Committee meeting on March 2. He said four of the interim matters involved the expansion of state public service posts under Article 112 of the Federal Constitution, as well as issues on health, education and the Borneonisation of the federal public service in Sabah and Sarawak. "The remaining 11 outstanding matters are being closely monitored by the Sabah and Sarawak Affairs Division, acting as secretariat, together with all stakeholders at federal and state levels," he said during question time at the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Media Prima launches Ruang Rehat initiative to promote mindful breaks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Media Prima Bhd has launched 'Ruang Rehat', a multi-platform social initiative aimed at combating stress and encouraging Malaysians to embrace mindful breaks as part of their daily lives. Anchored by the tagline 'Jaga Minda, Jaga Hati, Sayangi Diri', the initiative leverages Media Prima's integrated media ecosystem across television, radio, digital and out-of-home platforms to promote proactive mental wellness habits. Media Prima Bhd group managing director Datuk Rafiq Razali said the initiative aims to make mindful breaks a normal part of Malaysian life.

items.title: Yinson's net profit climbs 4.3pct to RM120mil in Q1
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Yinson Holdings Bhd's net profit climbed 4.3 per cent to RM120 million in the first quarter ended April 30, 2026 from RM115 million, supported by contributions from the Agogo floating production, storage and offloading (FPSO) vessel. However, revenue fell 14.7 per cent to RM1.05 billion from RM1.23 million a year ago. In a statement, Yinson group executive chairman Lim Han Weng said the steady quarterly performance was supported by long-term contracted revenues, a fully operational fleet, strengthened financial resilience and a diversified platform across offshore production, renewables and green technologies.

items.title: Matrade, CAIQ Biosecurity to support exporters for China market
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysia External Trade Development Corporation (Matrade) has entered into a collaboration agreement with CAIQ Biosecurity Sdn Bhd to enhance compliance assistance and improve market preparedness for Malaysian exporters seeking to expand into the Chinese market. The collaboration is implemented under the Malaysia-China Gateway Initiative (MCGi) to help Malaysian businesses better navigate China's import regulations. "As China remains a high-potential but technically demanding market, many Malaysian exporters in food, agriculture, cosmetics, health-related products and other regulated sectors require early preparation on product documentation, testing, labelling, registration and traceability requirements for market entry," Matrade said.

items.title: In beachside city, Lebanese try to shake off Israel-Hizbollah war
items.summary: HEAVY equipment operator Bassam Khalil's bulldozers and excavators are in high demand in south Lebanon's Tyre these days, as the seaside city tries to pick up the pieces from the latest Israel-Hizbollah war. Heavy Israeli airstrikes targeted Tyre, one of south Lebanon's largest cities, after Hizbollah dragged the country into the Middle East war on March 2 with rocket fire at Israel in support of its backer Iran. Tyre residents "love life and work.

items.title: Police reports lodged against Puad over claims of palace 'hand' in Johor politics
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Several Johor Barisan Nasional members lodged police reports against former Umno supreme council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi after he made allegations against the Johor palace and state Umno. The reports were lodged by MCA Youth chief Ling Tian Soon, Kulai BN division chief Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor, Umno Youth vice-chief and Umno supreme council member Mohd Hairi Mad Shah, Johor Baru Umno chief Datuk Yahya Jaafar and Johor Umno secretary Datuk Abdul Halim Suleiman. Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has refuted the claims .

items.title: Hamzah: If Bersatu turns adversarial, Wawasan will respond in kind
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Cinta Malaysia, now known as Parti Wawasan Negara, will assess Bersatu's position and statements before deciding the nature of its cooperation with the party, says its president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. Asked whether Wawasan would continue working with Bersatu within Perikatan Nasional (PN) following his expulsion from the party, Hamzah said he was monitoring developments closely. MORE TO COME © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Be more proactive’: Judge reprimands police over delay in locating Indira Gandhi’s ex-husband
items.summary: IPOH, June 25 — The High Court has granted police an additional six months to track down Muhammad Riduan Abdullah, the ex-husband of M. Indira Gandhi. The judge's remarks followed a submission by Indira’s lawyer, Harsimran Kaur, who highlighted a glaring delay in police action. Despite this lead, the police only moved to investigate on June 15—nearly two months later.

items.title: Asian stocks rebound as South Korea shares surge on AI optimism; currencies lag
items.summary: SINGAPORE: A surge in South Korean stocks helped a gauge tracking Asian equities to regain its footing on Thursday, with upbeat Micron Technology results reviving confidence in AI demand, while regional currencies weakened against a strong US dollar. South Korea's KOSPI index rose as much as six per cent, on track for its best session since June 12. Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix shares rose by 6.2 per cent and 11.6 per cent, respectively, on Thursday, as chipmakers rallied, tracking Micron Technology's upbeat quarterly results.

items.title: Indonesia sets 3-month transition period for B50 biodiesel programme, official says
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia has issued a regulation to implement its B50 biodiesel mandate starting from July 1, with a three-month transition period for retailers to clear their existing stocks, a senior energy ministry official said on Thursday. The launch of the B50 programme on July 1 means that all gasoil sold in the country must contain 50 per cent palm-based diesel and 50 per cent conventional diesel. The currently operating B40 policy stipulates that fuel must contain 40 per cent palm-based diesel.

items.title: Batik Air launches direct Kuala Lumpur-Shanghai service to boost China connectivity
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Batik Air has launched its new direct service between Kuala Lumpur and Shanghai, further strengthening its presence in China. The carrier said its inaugural flight departed Kuala Lumpur on Tuesday and arrived at Shanghai Pudong International Airport at 1.20 am the following morning, while the return service subsequently arrived at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) Terminal 1 at 8.50am yesterday. "The introduction of the Shanghai – Kuala Lumpur service comes at an opportune time as Malaysia intensifies efforts under the Visit Malaysia 2026 (VM2026) campaign.

items.title: UK's Reeves backs Burnham for prime minister, defers on own role
items.summary: LONDON: British finance minister Rachel Reeves said on Thursday she was backing Andy Burnham to be the next prime minister, brushing off reports she could lose her role and be moved to a more junior post if he took over the Labour Party. Reeves, a close ally of Starmer, said no one could doubt her commitment to the outgoing prime minister, adding that she had worked alongside him for six years. Asked about reports she could be demoted, Reeves said cabinet decisions would be a matter for Burnham.

items.title: Malaysia climbs to 12th spot in global peace rankings, among Asia’s safest nations
items.summary: Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the achievement places Malaysia amongst the safest countries in the Asia region alongside Singapore and Japan. He said the achievement reflects the continuous efforts of the Madani Government in ensuring that security, safety and stability remain the nation’s priorities. He said the Home Ministry would continue to strengthen efforts to enhance public safety, strengthen law enforcement, tighten control of national borders, combat crime and ensure public peace is always preserved.

items.title: Man found dead in parked car in George Town
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A 55-year-old man was found dead inside a parked car along Jalan Lintang Merdeka here today. The condition of the body indicated that the man had likely been dead for more than four days. Northeast district police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said police received a report at 9.50am after members of the public alerted authorities to an unconscious man inside a vehicle.

items.title: Kelantan Umno hits out at Puad Zarkashi after resignation
items.summary: KOTA BARU: The decision by Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Puad Zarkashi to quit the party reflects dissatisfaction and political immaturity. Kelantan Umno information chief Datuk Zawawi Othman said Puad's allegation that Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi was acting under instructions was baseless and failed to recognise the close working relationship between Umno and the royal institution in state administration. "Umno has always upheld and defended the institution of the Malay Rulers.

items.title: Johor polls: 15 Bersama candidates to declare assets through statutory declaration
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: All 15 Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) candidates contesting in the upcoming Johor election will declare their assets, income and expenditure through statutory declarations. In a statement today, Bersama said the candidates would also sign four statutory undertakings and a conditional letter of resignation, including agreement to a RM2 million penalty bond should they breach the anti-party-hopping pledge. "Details of assets, debts, income and expenditure for each candidate will be uploaded to the Bersama website for public access from 10pm tomorrow," the statement said.

items.title: Wawasan to skip Johor polls, contest Negri Sembilan election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Parti Cinta Malaysia, now known as Parti Wawasan Negara, will not contest the Johor state election but will support Perikatan Nasional, says its newly appointed president Datuk Seri Hamzah Zainudin. The Larut MP said, however, that the party would contest the Negri Sembilan election, adding that its participation had been confirmed following his election as party president at its annual general meeting on June 20. "We will contest Negri Sembilan.

items.title: Mindef launches new defence strategy amid evolving global threats
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Defence Ministry (Mindef) has developed two strategic plans to respond to evolving global threats. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the two documents, the National Defence Strategic Plan (NDSP) and the Defence Capacity Blueprint (DCB), will guide Malaysia's defence priorities from 2026 to 2030. "Which are capability empowerment and army readiness, strengthened defence capacity, workforce well-being, civil-military cooperation for total defence, science, technology and industry development.

items.title: PRG shareholders reject three key AGM resolutions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Shareholders of PRG Holdings Bhd voted against three key resolutions at the company's annual general meeting (AGM) today. Datuk Sheah Kok Fah, PRG's second-largest shareholder with an 8.14 per cent stake, said the outcome sends a strong message that shareholders are concerned about the company's governance, transparency and accountability standards. On the AGM outcome, Sheah said shareholders had clearly expressed concerns over governance and disclosure practices within PRG.

items.title: Man suffers burns, 3 injured in LPG fire at Pasir Gudang flat
items.summary: PASIR GUDANG: A man suffered burns and three family members were injured in a fire believed to have been caused by a leaking LPG hose at their Taman Air Biru home last night. A Johor Fire and Rescue Department spokesperson said the 30-year-old man sustained burns to 20 per cent of his legs and 50 per cent of his hands in the blaze at the kitchen of a third-floor unit at Block 14, SEDC Flats. The department received an emergency call at 9.26pm, with 10 firefighters, a Fire and Rescue Tender unit and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services team from the Pasir Gudang station arriving at 9.31pm.

items.title: Sedition charge for Chegubard over social media post on Negeri Sembilan royalty
items.summary: SEREMBAN, June 25 — Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to a charge of publishing seditious material against the Negeri Sembilan Royal Institution via his Facebook account last month. Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Sazali Omar proposed bail at RM10,000 and applied for the court to issue a gag order prohibiting Badrul Hisham from making any statements concerning the Negeri Sembilan Royal Institution. However, Badrul Hisham’s lawyer, Muhammad Rafique Rashid Ali, applied for bail to be reduced to RM2,500 and objected to the gag order sought against his client.

items.title: 11 developers registered with CRESS as of May 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 11 developers have registered under the Corporate Renewable Energy Supply Scheme (CRESS) as of May 2026. The number includes eight green consumers from the data centre, high-technology and manufacturing sectors, involving a combined renewable energy (RE) capacity of about three gigawatts, said Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Energy Transition and Water Transformation Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. "Their participation reflects positive interest from industry players in the direct green electricity supply mechanism, particularly to meet the needs of large-scale corporate consumers," he said during the Minister's Question Time in the Dewan Rakyat today.

items.title: Man arrested hours after mosque handbag robbery in Penang
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: A 54-year-old man was arrested hours after allegedly robbing a woman of her handbag during dawn prayers at a mosque here yesterday. Northeast district police chief Assistant Commissioner Abdul Rozak Muhammad said police received a report at 8.41am yesterday from a 48-year-old woman claiming her handbag was snatched by an unknown man at a mosque along Jalan Kapitan Keling. Abdul Rozak said police also recovered the victim's belongings from the suspect during the arrest.

items.title: Malaysia developing national water reclamation policy to power data centre growth, says Fadillah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The government is undertaking a comprehensive study to establish policy and infrastructure foundations for a national water reclamation policy to support the development of data centres, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof. Fadillah, who is also Energy Transition and Water Transformation Minister, said Malaysia currently produces 48.5 million litres per day of reclaimed water and is targeting 118 million litres per day by 2030 for industrial use. On the supply side, Fadillah said Malaysia has surpassed 13.3 gigawatts of installed renewable energy capacity as of 2025, with solar energy recording the fastest growth.

items.title: BN new faces vow strong push for Johor victory in state election
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: New faces from Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) component parties are determined to deliver victory for the coalition in the 16th state election on July 11. BN candidate for Bukit Kepong, Ahmad Syar'e Yusof, 51, said although this marked his first time contesting, he was confident of reclaiming the seat for the coalition. "As a new candidate, I want to ensure continuity of plans under the previous state administration, including the Maju Johor 2030 agenda," he said after the BN candidate announcement ceremony at the Johor Umno liaison office yesterday.

items.title: Police report lodged over Puad's claims linking Johor palace to state assembly dissolution
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: A police report has been lodged over statements by former Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi , who allegedly linked the Johor palace to the dissolution of the state assembly and claimed that Johor Umno was under royal influence. MCA Youth chief Ling Tian Soon lodged the report after Puad allegedly claimed that caretaker Johor menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi had told him the state assembly was dissolved on the palace's "instructions." Ling also said Puad had alleged that Johor Umno was being controlled by the palace.

items.title: Chegubard pleads not guilty to sedition over Negri royal institution remarks
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Political activist Badrul Hisham Shaharin, better known as Chegubard, pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to publishing seditious content on Facebook allegedly directed at the Negri Sembilan Royal Institution. According to the charge, Badrul Hisham allegedly published the content on Facebook under the account "Che GuBard" about 8.30pm on May 26 in Kuala Pilah. Deputy public prosecutor Datuk Ahmad Sazali Omar proposed bail of RM10,000 and applied for a gag order barring the accused from making statements relating to the Negri Sembilan Royal Institution.

items.title: Malaysia to roll out water reclamation policy to support industrial demand, data centres
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is drafting a National Water Reclamation Policy to expand the use of recycled water for industrial activities, including data centres, while preserving treated water supplies for households. "We have a lot of water. The issue is separating water for consumption from water for industry," he said.

items.title: Onn Hafiz hits back at Puad Zarkashi, says royal consent is constitutional process not royal instruction
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 25 — Johor Umno Liaison Committee chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has strongly denied allegations by former Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi that he had said the palace had ‘ordered’ the dissolution of the Johor legislative assembly. The Menteri Besar said the dissolution was carried out following the consent of Johor Regent Tunku Mahkota Ismail, in accordance with Article 23, Second Part of the Laws of the State of Johor. After receiving consent, I announced the dissolution of the state legislative assembly to the people.” “Obtaining royal consent is a constitutional process and in no way implies receiving instructions or political interference,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: New Pahang police HQ project back on track after land issues resolved
items.summary: KUANTAN: Construction of the new Pahang police contingent headquarters in Sultan Ahmad Shah Administrative Centre (KotaSAS) here will resume after issues involving the project site were resolved. Outgoing Pahang police chief Datuk Seri Yahaya Othman, who retired today, is succeeded by his deputy, Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob, in an acting capacity. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim announced in Budget 2025 an allocation for the new Pahang police headquarters, alongside a district police headquarters in Petaling Jaya and several police stations nationwide.

items.title: Indonesia weighs US$2 billion cut to Prabowo's signature free meals programme
items.summary: JAKARTA: Indonesia is preparing to scale back President Prabowo Subianto's flagship free meals programme, with officials weighing another budget cut of more than US$2 billion along with reductions in kitchens and beneficiaries, according to two sources familiar with the matter and an internal presentation. Two sources familiar with the plans told Reuters the National Nutrition Agency (NNA), which runs the programme, is targeting a reduction of at least 15 per cent from its 268 trillion rupiah (US$16 billion) budget this year - equivalent to about 40 trillion rupiah - after identifying inefficiencies. One of them said the cut could be 50 trillion rupiah.

items.title: Haim Hilman charged with making statements likely to cause public alarm
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Jitra assemblyman Dr Haim Hilman Abdullah pleaded not guilty at the magistrate's court here today to a charge of making statements likely to cause public alarm. The Kedah executive councillor for Industry and Investment, Higher Education, Science, Technology and Innovation entered the plea before magistrate Nurul Saqinah Rosli. According to the charge, Haim Hilman, 53, allegedly made statements with intent to cause public alarm or disturb public order between 8pm and midnight on June 13 at the Pas Seremban headquarters along Jalan Jelebu.

items.title: Malaysia does not recognise Myanmar administration, says Tok Mat
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia has never recognised Myanmar's post-election administration, and recent engagement with officials in Naypyidaw should not be construed as diplomatic recognition. Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan said his visit to Myanmar in May was part of Asean's efforts to maintain communication channels and advance peace initiatives under the Five-Point Consensus (5PC), not an endorsement of the country's leadership. Mohamad said Malaysia's stance remained unchanged despite his meeting with Myanmar Foreign Minister U Than Swe during a two-day visit to Naypyidaw from May 19 to 20.

items.title: Bankrupt man claims trial over acting as company director
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A bankrupt man has claimed trial at the Sessions Court here to a charge of acting as a company director without court approval. In a statement today, the Companies Commission of Malaysia (SSM) reminded the public that enforcement action would be taken against individuals found breaching provisions under laws regulated by the commission. Yusri Husain pleaded not guilty before Judge Roslan Hamid on June 18 to an offence under Section 198(1)(a) of the Companies Act 2016.

items.title: Umno sec-gen alleges Puad Zarkashi quit because his son was not picked for Johor election
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Umno secretary-general Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki has responded to the resignation of Datuk Mohd Puad Zarkashi, claiming the decision was motivated by a disagreement over candidate selection for the Rengit state seat. Asyraf’s remarks follow Puad’s announcement earlier today that he was leaving the party effective immediately. Puad, the former Johor State Assembly Speaker, had cited alleged external interference in the dissolution of the Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 1 as his primary reason for exiting.

items.title: Malaysia acquires French CAESAR artillery systems, eyes homegrown weapons for ANKA-S fleet, says Khaled
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Malaysia has formally procured CAESAR 155mm self-propelled howitzers (SPH) from France, Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin confirmed today. That will be another asset added to our arsenal,” he told reporters after launching the National Defence Strategic Plan (PSPN) and the National Defence Capacity Action Plan at Royale Chulan Kuala Lumpur today. However, Khaled said details on the delivery timeline would be announced later and stressed that the procurement process would be carried out transparently.

items.title: MyKad-linked diesel subsidy reforms could save Putrajaya up to RM2b annually, says Amir Hamzah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Putrajaya’s pivot towards using the MyKad for subsidised diesel and uniformed pricing could save the government up to RM2 billion a year, Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan told Parliament this morning. Subsidised diesel leakage persisted despite efforts to curb smuggling. The subsidised rate of diesel in the two states was RM2.15 per litre.

items.title: Indira Gandhi fails in second bid to cite IGP for contempt over daughter recovery case
items.summary: IPOH, June 25 — The High Court here today dismissed M. Indira Gandhi’s application for leave to initiate contempt of court proceedings against the Inspector-General of Police (IGP). “As such, the court finds no prima facie evidence of deliberate disobedience,” she said. The High Court dismissed the application on Aug 2, 2022.

items.title: Govt rolls out RM710m package, RM10b guarantees to cushion job losses and support SMEs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The government has rolled out a RM710 million labour market resilience package alongside RM10 billion in SME financing guarantees to cushion job losses and support businesses amid global economic uncertainty, including energy price pressures and supply chain disruptions, Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said. He said the measures, coordinated through the National Economic Action Council (MTEN), form part of an integrated approach to protect workers, sustain businesses and stabilise the labour market. Akmal also said Malaysia’s labour market had remained resilient despite external pressures, supported by these interventions.

items.title: Nothing ‘personal’: Malaysian Bar not against Zahid, Najib; court challenges law-based, president says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The Malaysian Bar, a professional body which represents all of Peninsular Malaysia’s lawyers, is not political or partisan, and instead makes its stand based on the law, its president Anand Raj said. Directly addressing the Malaysian Bar’s court challenges or positions in cases involving Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Zahid Hamidi and former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak, he said these challenges are based on legal principles. — Picture by Sayuti Zainudin Malaysian Bar took position that favoured Najib too, because it’s not about him As for Najib, Anand said the Malaysian Bar had taken positions on law that could be seen as against and favouring the former prime minister in different cases, as it is simply not about him.

items.title: Kedah exco Haim Hilman claims trial over PAS ceramah statement on university admissions
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Kedah state executive councillor Haim Hilman Abdullah pleaded not guilty today to a charge of making a statement during a PAS ceramah that could potentially cause public fear or alarm, according to The Star. The Jitra assemblyman claimed trial after the charge was read out before Magistrate Nurul Saqinah Rosli at the Magistrate’s Court here today. State prosecution chief Ku Hayati Ku Haron offered bail of RM10,000 pending the next mention and requested that Haim Hilman surrender his passport to the court.

items.title: Selangor Education Department defers Bangi student drowning case to authorities
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 25 — The Selangor State Education Department (JPN) has handed over the investigation into the case of a secondary school student believed to have drowned in Bangi yesterday to the relevant authorities. The department extended its condolences to the student’s family and hoped that they would be given strength and fortitude in facing this difficult time. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the department received an emergency call regarding the incident at 4.23 pm.

items.title: Puad Zarkashi exits Umno ahead of Johor state election
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 25 — Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi announced his departure from the party with immediate effect today. “Without any hesitation, I announce my departure from Umno with immediate effect from today, June 25, 2026. Mohd Puad, who is also a former Johor State Legislative Assembly Speaker, dismissed suggestions that his decision was driven by personal interests.

items.title: Police seek public’s help to trace missing woman last seen leaving Kamunting home for job interview
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Police are seeking the public’s assistance to locate a 41-year-old woman who has been reported missing in Taiping since June 16, according to Sinar Harian . Acting Taiping police chief Supt Mohd Ariffridzuan Ezahar said police received a report on the disappearance of Nor Afiza Zainuddin at 11.22am on June 18. Mohd Ariffridzuan said police later found the Perodua Axia at a recreational area in Kampung Paya Lintang, Batu Hampar near Padang Rengas on June 21.

items.title: Ketamine and Erimin 5 ring smashed in Perak, 17-year-old among three suspects
items.summary: IPOH, June 25 — Police arrested three individuals, including a 17-year-old, after dismantling a drug trafficking syndicate involving ketamine and Erimin 5 pills worth RM120,050 in Pengkalan Tiara here on Monday. Perak police chief Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin said the Narcotics Crime Investigation Department (NCID) of the Perak Contingent Police Headquarters (IPK) carried out the raid at 8.45 pm after receiving intelligence on drug trafficking activities in the area. Mohd Alwi said initial urine screening tests showed that one suspect tested positive for ketamine, while the other two tested negative.

items.title: Melaka Form Two student dies after collapsing during rugby training, says state exco
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — A Form Two student died after collapsing during a rugby training session at a school in Telok Mas, Melaka yesterday, according to Sinar Harian . The victim’s grand-uncle, Yahya Endut, 64, said he was shocked by the incident involving the teenager, whom he had helped raise since childhood. Meanwhile, Melaka education, higher education and religious affairs committee chairman Datuk Rahmad Mariman confirmed the incident and said the student collapsed during a post-training briefing before being pronounced dead.

items.title: Zambry: USM and UKM lead global charge in UN sustainability goals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) and Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) have put Malaysia on the global map after securing places among the world’s top seven universities in the Times Higher Education (THE) Impact Rankings 2026. Higher Education Minister Datuk Seri Dr Zambry Abdul Kadir said USM was ranked fifth globally and UKM seventh among more than 1,600 universities assessed for their performance in advancing the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). He said USM also retained its top global position in SDG 17: Partnerships for the Goals, highlighting Malaysian universities’ ability to lead international strategic cooperation.

items.title: Bund breach floods 32 homes in Kuala Langat housing area, residents urged to move to safety
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — A total of 32 homes in Taman Langat Utama 2, Bukit Changgang, Kuala Langat, were affected by flooding after a bund reportedly breached late last night, according to Sinar Harian. Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant operations director Ashrul Riezal Asbar said firefighters received an emergency call regarding the incident at 4.12am. “Upon arrival, firefighters found that floodwaters had inundated the residential area,” he said in a statement today.

items.title: Is Battersea Power Station overvalued? MOF reveals forensic audit findings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — An independent auditor had submitted two forensic reports regarding allegations about the Battersea Power Station project’s overvaluation of investment assets, concluding that the claims raised are unfounded, and therefore, there is no need for further investigation into Battersea Project Holding Company, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said. The ministry said Battersea Project Holding Company had appointed independent forensic auditor Evelyn Partners to review the allegations concerning the project’s financial statements. The MOF added that the Battersea Project Holding Company’s accounts were also audited and certified by PwC, one of the Big Four accounting firms.

items.title: Malaysia’s fuel subsidy bill triples to RM11b amid West Asia crisis, MOF says
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 25 — The BUDI MADANI targeted fuel subsidy programme had benefited 14 million Malaysians and involved 11.1 billion litres of fuel as of May 31, 2026, the Ministry of Finance (MOF) said. MOF said the sales value totalled RM22.1 billion, of which RM11.2 billion was borne by the government as a subsidy. It said that since the advent of the West Asia crisis, BUDI MADANI subsidy spending has increased, on average, by threefold compared with the same period in 2025.

items.title: These 21 Johor seats decided two elections — Will they decide a third on July 11?
items.summary: Gambir, once won by Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin under the PH banner, reverted to BN. Similarly, Parit Yaani returned to the BN column after unseating Johor PH chairman Aminolhuda Hassan. The 21 seats that flipped in 2022: PH to BN Pemanis (PKR to Umno) Kemelah (Amanah to MIC) Tenang (Bersatu to Umno) Gambir (Bersatu to Umno) Serom (Amanah to Umno) Bukit Naning (PKR to Umno) Semerah (PKR to Umno) Yong Peng (DAP to MCA) Parit Yaani (Amanah to Umno) Senggarang (Amanah to Umno) Permas (Bersatu to Umno) Larkin (Bersatu to Umno) Kempas (Bersatu to Umno) Bukit Permai (Bersatu to Umno) Pulai Sebatang (Amanah to Umno) PH to PN Maharani (Amanah to PAS) BN to PN Endau (Umno to Bersatu) PH (Bersatu) to PN (Bersatu) Bukit Kepong (Retained by Sahruddin Jamal, but shifted from PH to PN following Bersatu’s exit) The turnout factor The political shift was inseparable from a collapse in voter participation.

items.title: Fuel prices drop: Finance ministry announces lowered petrol and diesel rates amid easing global market
items.summary: Meanwhile, diesel in Peninsular Malaysia will see a 30 sen decrease, bringing the price down to RM4.07 per litre. Other subsidised rates remain at RM2.15 per litre for diesel in Sabah, Sarawak, and Labuan, RM2.05 per litre under the Subsidised Petrol Control System (SKPS), and RM2.15 per litre under the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS). Retail price Fuel Retail price Changes RON97 RM4.10 per litre (24 Jun 2026 price: RM4.35 per litre) Down 25 sen per litre RON95 RM3.47 per litre (24 Jun 2026 price: RM3.72 per litre) Down 25 sen per litre Diesel (Peninsular Malaysia) RM4.07 per litre (24 Jun 2026 price: RM4.37 per litre) Down 30 sen per litre Current maintained subsidised prices as follows: Fuel Subsidised price RON95 (BUDI95) RM1.99 per litre Diesel (Sabah, Sarawak dan W.P Labuan) RM2.15 per litre Sistem Kawalan Petrol Bersubsidi (SKPS) RM2.05 per litre Sistem Kawalan Diesel Bersubsidi (SKDS) RM2.15 per litre

items.title: Occupational safety department investigating fatal accident at Sungai Buloh water tank cleaning site
items.summary: Hazlina said the investigation was being carried out under Sections 15, 17 and 18 of the Occupational Safety and Health Act 1994, which stipulate the responsibilities of employers, self-employed persons and relevant parties to ensure the safety, health and welfare of workers and others who may be affected by work activities. She reminded employers and workers to comply with occupational safety and health requirements, particularly when carrying out work in confined spaces. “Employers must prioritise the safety and health of all individuals involved in work activities, including vendors and contractors,” she said.

items.title: Education minister files police report over AI-generated video, calling it malicious and defamatory
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Parti Keadilan Rakyat (PKR) Wanita chief Fadhlina Sidek has filed a police report over the circulation of an AI-generated video spreading false allegations against her. Fadhlina, who also serves as Education Minister, expressed regret in a statement today over the video’s content, which she described as malicious. She also called on all parties to adopt a firm position on issues of slander, character assassination and sexual harassment targeting women politicians.

items.title: Going it alone: Johor BN will not form a joint government with any coalition, says Onn Hafiz
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 24 — The Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) will not form a coalition government with any other political party if it manages to retain power after the state election, said its state chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi. He said the Johor BN’s stance remains firm despite facing criticism since announcing that coalition would contest all 56 state assembly seats for the upcoming state polls on May 16. “This decision was made based on differences in approach and ideology,” he said in his address at the BN Johor state electron candidate announcement ceremony held at the Johor Umno state liaison office here today.

items.title: BN announces Johor election candidate lists, Onn Hafiz spearheading the charge
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, June 24 — Barisan Nasional (BN) announced its list of 56 candidates for the Johor election today, ending speculation over which candidates the coalition would field. Johor BN chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi announced the list of candidates for the coalition that will contest in the upcoming Johor election. Present were respective component party and affiliate group leaders such as MCA president Datuk Seri Wee Ka Siong; MIC president Tan Sri S.A. Vigneswaran; People’s Progressive Party (PPP) president Datuk Dr J. Loga Bala Mohan and Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh.

items.title: Not out to get you: New Section 42A meant to rein in illegal racing, dangerous speed trials
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke has clarified that the new Section 42A under the Road Transport (Amendment) Bill 2026 is not intended to penalise isolated speeding or ordinary overtaking. Instead, he explained that it targets illegal racing and dangerous speed trials involving competitive conduct. “It targets racing or speed trials involving competitive conduct, challenge based speeding, reckless behaviour, or situations that clearly indicate dangerous activity.

items.title: As traffic violations rise, so must the penalties: Loke defends RM500 fine hike
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 24 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke today defended his ministry’s push to raise the penalty for traffic violations to RM500, amid some pushback from Opposition lawmakers who said the move could disproportionately penalise lower income road users. MOT had proposed the increment as part of several amendments to the Road Transport Act tabled Monday, as Loke vowed to impose harsher penalties amid concerns over the rising cases of non-compliance and road safety. Loke explained that the authorities can still impose a lower fine for a violation on a case-to-case basis, countering allegations that the increment would lump lesser serious offences together with more serious violations.

items.title: E-hailing driver fined RM101,750 for importing 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water
items.summary: KLANG, June 24 — An e-hailing driver was fined RM101,750 by the Magistrate’s Court here today after he was found guilty of importing prohibited goods, namely 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water through West Port, Klang, five years ago. He was also ordered to pay customs duty amounting to 20 per cent of the goods’ value, totalling RM2,035, to the Government through the Royal Malaysian Customs Department (JKDM) under Section 119A of the Customs Act 1967. The court further ordered all 5,500 bottles of zam-zam water to be forfeited to JKDM under Section 127 of the same Act.
